purpose: Independent corroboration of AURA's vision discrimination against a separately-trained public model (torchxrayvision densenet121-res224-mimic_ch) on the same images. Because xrv's training labels are independent of AURA's regex labels, agreement here is evidence the model is not merely fitting its own label noise.

provenance.sampling: uniform random per-study MIMIC-CXR validation images, seed 20260722
provenance.operating_point_for_kappa: prevalence-matched: each model thresholded to the finding's target base rate (TARGET_PREV) so kappa measures agreement on WHICH images, not calibration offset. Both marginals equal, so kappa is not inflated by a base-rate mismatch.
provenance.why_not_op_threshs: xrv's shipped op_threshs for mimic_ch are 0.005-0.11; naive 'prob>=op_thresh' flags ~100% of images positive (a known xrv absolute-calibration quirk), which makes kappa degenerate. Prevalence-matching avoids relying on either model's absolute calibration.

excluded_findings.hyperinflation: no hyperinflation/emphysema pathology in densenet121-res224-mimic_ch; no independent label to compare

provenance.metric_notes: PRIMARY evidence is threshold-free: spearman_rho_probs (rank correlation of the two models' probabilities) and the auroc_* columns (each model's score discriminating the other's independent calls). These need no operating point. cohen_kappa_prevalence_matched is the requested binary-agreement statistic at a common base rate.

interpretation: Substantial per-finding kappa AND high threshold-free rank agreement between two independently-labelled DenseNets is direct evidence against the circularity objection: AURA's scores track an external model that never saw AURA's regex labels. Kappa is attenuated for rare findings (nodule/pneumothorax) by low prevalence, where the threshold-free AUROC is the fairer read.
